predictions.rationale: Portugal are ~74% favourites with a deep squad and Ronaldo targeting this opener. DR Congo — returning to the World Cup for the first time since 1974 — are athletic and organised but a clear tier below. Portugal's possession and quality should control the game; their defensive record (only 5 clean sheets in last 20) suggests DR Congo may threaten but shouldn't stop a comfortable Portugal win.

predictions.rationale: England are ~56% favourites and arrive in flawless qualifying form (8 wins, 22-0 goals, 8 clean sheets). Croatia's ageing midfield — Modrić, Kovačić, Perišić — has conceded in 6 consecutive matches. Tino Livramento is injured but England's attacking depth remains formidable. Croatia's tournament know-how keeps the draw price (26%) very much alive, but England's superior quality should edge it.

predictions.rationale: Thomas Partey — Ghana's captain and midfield anchor — has been denied a Canadian entry visa and will miss this match entirely. Ghana were already in dismal form (winless in 7, losses to Austria 5-1 and Germany 2-1). Panama qualified CONCACAF unbeaten (7W 3D) and conceded just 4 in 8 qualifying games. The Opta Supercomputer (before factoring in Partey's absence) already gave Panama 45.2% vs Ghana 29.6% — this concrete, breaking development tips the balance further toward Panama.
predictions.disagreement: The market still has Ghana at 41% — likely not yet fully pricing in Partey's absence. Lean to Panama despite the near-equal odds.

Colombia have never drawn 0-0 in 22 World Cup matches; backing them to win with margin.

predictions.rationale: Both teams arrive with 3 points and near-equal market probability. South Korea came from behind to beat Czech Republic 2-1 with Son Heung-min and Lee Kang-in in excellent form. The draw is the modal tipster pick and reflects the genuine competitiveness here — backing a tight, cagey affair with both sides protecting group leads.

Brazil drew Morocco 1-1 in their opener but that was a much closer test — Haiti is a different proposition entirely. Neymar is targeting this match after injury. Backing a comfortable Brazil rout.
